seo.title: Remix tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Remix tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of essential tools, libraries, and deployment platforms for building full-stack applications with Remix and React Router v7.

entries.name: React Router v7
entries.description: The unified core of Remix and React Router, providing the framework for routing, data loading, and mutations.
pros: Unified codebase for SPA and SSR applications
Automatic code splitting and data prefetching
cons: Steep learning curve for developers coming from client-only React

entries.name: Prisma
entries.description: Next-generation ORM with an auto-generated query builder and type-safe migrations for Remix backends.
pros: Auto-generated TypeScript types based on schema
Excellent Studio UI for data exploration
cons: Can have cold-start issues on serverless functions
Large binary size compared to lighter alternatives

entries.name: Drizzle ORM
entries.description: Headless TypeScript ORM with a focus on maximum performance and zero overhead, ideal for edge functions.
pros: Extremely lightweight with no heavy binaries
Native support for Cloudflare D1 and SQLite

entries.name: Conform
entries.description: A type-safe form validation library designed specifically for the Remix/React Router progressive enhancement model.
pros: Handles complex nested objects and arrays
cons: API can be verbose for simple forms

entries.name: Remix Auth
entries.description: A strategy-based authentication library for Remix, modeled after Passport.js but built for the Web Fetch API.
pros: Works with standard Remix cookie sessions
cons: Requires manual setup of session storage

entries.name: The Epic Stack
entries.description: A comprehensive opinionated project starter by Kent C. Dodds including SQLite, Prisma, and Fly.io deployment.
pros: Includes pre-configured testing with Vitest and Playwright

entries.name: Fly.io
entries.description: Public cloud platform that runs applications close to users, optimized for Remix's Node.js or Docker deployments.
pros: Native support for multi-region SQLite via LiteFS
cons: Requires Docker knowledge for custom configurations
Pricing can be unpredictable with high traffic

entries.name: Cloudflare Workers
entries.description: Edge computing platform that allows Remix applications to run globally with zero cold starts.
pros: Extremely low latency via global edge network
cons: Requires specific adapters for database connections

entries.name: Vitest
entries.description: A Vite-native unit test framework used for testing Remix loaders, actions, and utilities.
cons: Can require complex mocking for browser-only globals

entries.name: Remix Development Tools
entries.description: A browser extension and library for debugging Remix routes, loaders, and state management.
pros: Visualizes route hierarchy and data flow
Inspects active loaders and action data
cons: Occasional UI glitches with complex nested routes
